forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+project: file-edit-tools
+title: Inline diagnostics on edit never establishes a leader (follower doesn't elect/warm, NotLeader silently swallowed) — "does not work at all"
+---
+## What
+The inline-diagnostics fold-in (the edit tool's "primary trigger and delivery surface for diagnostics") never actually produces diagnostics in a normal MCP process, and hides the fact that it didn't. A breaking edit and a genuinely-clean edit return byte-identical envelopes. Reproduced live: editing a file to a compile error yields no diagnostics/pending block; a second breaking edit does NOT trigger it either — nothing in the edit→diagnose flow bootstraps a leader, so repeated edits keep hitting the same unreachable path.
+
+Cross-project note: this spans `swissarmyhammer-tools` (the edit/inline path), `swissarmyhammer-diagnostics`, `swissarmyhammer-lsp` (the single rust-analyzer session), and `swissarmyhammer-leader-election`. Filed under `file-edit-tools` because the edit tool is the trigger/delivery surface; coordinate with the `diagnostics` project.
+
+### Root cause (verified)
+1. **Pure-consumer diagnose path — never elects/warms.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/diagnostics/mod.rs::produce_outcome` (lines 365-411): finds an in-process LSP session via `lsp_session_for_file`/`any_lsp_session`; if there is none it calls `diagnose_via_leader` (lines 427-443), which only *connects* a `SessionRequestClient` to an already-bound leader socket (`workspace.socket_path()`/`lock_path()`). Neither branch spawns an in-process session nor elects/spawns a leader. A fresh stdio MCP process (no warm session, no bound leader) gets `IpcError::NotLeader` and there is no code path that bootstraps one — so edits can never make diagnostics appear.
+2. **Silent swallow.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs::LiveDiagnoser::diagnose` (lines 54-74) catches that typed `NotLeader` error, logs it at `debug!`, and returns `DiagnoseOutcome { report: empty, pending: false }`. `fold_outcome_into_result` then attaches nothing — indistinguishable from "code is clean." Note the irony: `produce_outcome`'s own unit test `produce_outcome_without_a_session_surfaces_the_typed_not_leader_error` (mod.rs:1001) asserts the typed error MUST surface, but the inline wrapper swallows it at the one place a human/model sees it.
+
+## Required behavior (what the user demanded)
+- **A leader-capable process must lead.** On the first mutating edit (or at server start), the process must be able to elect itself leader and warm the single rust-analyzer session, so a diagnose request has somewhere to go. "Not trying to connect/elect" is the core defect.
+- **A follower must find a leader.** When another process already holds leadership, the follower's `diagnose_via_leader` must reach it and fold real diagnostics in (this connect path exists but is currently untested end-to-end and never exercised because no leader is ever elected in the common single-process case).
+- **When it genuinely cannot diagnose, say so — don't fake clean.** In `LiveDiagnoser`'s error arm, instead of an empty outcome, fold a typed marker into the envelope (e.g. `diagnostics_unavailable: "could not reach LSP leader"`) so an empty result distinguishes "clean" from "diagnoser never ran." (This observability slice is small and independently shippable; land it + a failing real-path test first.)
+
+## Approach
+- Make the edit→diagnose path establish a reachable diagnoser: on first mutating call, elect/become leader (via `swissarmyhammer-leader-election`) and spawn/warm the shared `swissarmyhammer-lsp` rust-analyzer session for the workspace, so `produce_outcome` finds an in-process session (or a reachable leader). Reuse the existing leader/session machinery the `diagnostics` MCP tool and code-context use — do not spawn a second LSP client (single-client invariant).
+- Surface the typed marker in `inline_diagnostics.rs` rather than swallowing.
+- Keep the fold-in best-effort (never fail the underlying edit), but "best-effort" must mean "attach a reason," not "silently drop."
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] After editing a file to a real compile error through the MCP `files edit` tool, the tool result envelope contains diagnostics for that error (not an empty/clean envelope) — in a single-process server with no pre-warmed session.
+- [ ] When the diagnoser truly cannot run (no leader electable, e.g. forced), the envelope carries a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` reason — never byte-identical to a clean result.
+- [ ] A follower process (leadership already held elsewhere) reaches the leader and folds the leader's real diagnostics into its edit result.
+- [ ] No second rust-analyzer/LSP client is spawned (single-client invariant preserved); the edit still succeeds even if diagnostics fail.
+
+## Tests (the missing coverage the user called out — real-path, not mocks)
+- [ ] **Leader-capable process leads:** a test that starts from no bound leader, performs a mutating edit on a broken file, and asserts a leader/session is established and real diagnostics are folded in (end-to-end through the production fold-in, not a stubbed `MutationDiagnoser`).
+- [ ] **Follower finds leader:** with a leader already bound (its socket/lock present), a follower's `produce_outcome`/`diagnose_via_leader` returns the leader's real report and the envelope carries it.
+- [ ] **Unavailable is observable:** force the no-leader-reachable case and assert the envelope carries the typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker (regression for the silent-swallow at inline_diagnostics.rs:63-72) — this test FAILS today.
+- [ ] Gate any test that needs a real rust-analyzer behind the usual LSP-available guard (skip cleanly if rust-analyzer isn't installed), but the leader-election/marker logic must be covered without requiring the binary.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`); `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "edit-broken-file → diagnostics appear" real-path test and the `diagnostics_unavailable` marker test FIRST (both fail today), then implement. If the leader-election/warm-up portion exceeds sizing, split it out and land the observability marker + failing real-path test as the prerequisite slice.
\ No newline at end of file

A fresh stdio MCP process (no warm session, no bound leader) gets `IpcError::NotLeader` and there is no code path that bootstraps one — so edits can never make diagnostics appear.
-2. **Silent swallow.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs::LiveDiagnoser::diagnose` (lines 54-74) catches that typed `NotLeader` error, logs it at `debug!`, and returns `DiagnoseOutcome { report: empty, pending: false }`. `fold_outcome_into_result` then attaches nothing — indistinguishable from "code is clean." (This observability slice is small and independently shippable; land it + a failing real-path test first.)

Reuse the existing leader/session machinery the `diagnostics` MCP tool and code-context use — do not spawn a second LSP client (single-client invariant).
-- Surface the typed marker in `inline_diagnostics.rs` rather than swallowing.
-- Keep the fold-in best-effort (never fail the underlying edit), but "best-effort" must mean "attach a reason," not "silently drop."
-
-## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] When the diagnoser truly cannot run (no leader electable, e.g. forced), the envelope carries a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` reason — never byte-identical to a clean result.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -4,39 +4,54 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a180
 project: file-edit-tools
-title: Inline diagnostics on edit never establishes a leader (follower doesn't elect/warm, NotLeader silently swallowed) — "does not work at all"
+title: Diagnostics quiescence declares "settled/clean" while rust-analyzer is still loading/indexing — can't distinguish clean from not-analyzed-yet
 ---
 ## What
-The inline-diagnostics fold-in (the edit tool's "primary trigger and delivery surface for diagnostics") never actually produces diagnostics in a normal MCP process, and hides the fact that it didn't. A breaking edit and a genuinely-clean edit return byte-identical envelopes. Reproduced live: editing a file to a compile error yields no diagnostics/pending block; a second breaking edit does NOT trigger it either — nothing in the edit→diagnose flow bootstraps a leader, so repeated edits keep hitting the same unreachable path.
+The diagnostics system cannot tell "the code is clean" from "rust-analyzer hasn't analyzed it yet" — both render as 0 errors with `pending: false`. After an edit that introduces a real compile error (E0308), the inline fold-in AND the explicit `diagnostics` tool both return empty-and-not-pending, so the model/user sees "looks fine" on genuinely-broken code.

-Cross-project note: this spans `swissarmyhammer-tools` (the edit/inline path), `swissarmyhammer-diagnostics`, `swissarmyhammer-lsp` (the single rust-analyzer session), and `swissarmyhammer-leader-election`. Filed under `file-edit-tools` because the edit tool is the trigger/delivery surface; coordinate with the `diagnostics` project.
+### Evidence (live repro — supersedes an earlier WRONG hypothesis)
+An earlier diagnosis blamed leader-election ("follower can't reach/elect a leader; NotLeader silently swallowed"). **That was wrong for the observed case and should not be chased as the root cause:**
+- `list servers` showed rust-analyzer running (fresh pid), and the explicit `diagnostics` check returned `0 errors` **empty — NOT** a "could not reach leader" error. So the leader was reachable and a session existed.
+- A second breaking edit produced an identical empty envelope; waiting 12s and re-checking still gave 0 errors.

-### Root cause (verified)
-1. **Pure-consumer diagnose path — never elects/warms.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/diagnostics/mod.rs::produce_outcome` (lines 365-411): finds an in-process LSP session via `lsp_session_for_file`/`any_lsp_session`; if there is none it calls `diagnose_via_leader` (lines 427-443), which only *connects* a `SessionRequestClient` to an already-bound leader socket (`workspace.socket_path()`/`lock_path()`). Neither branch spawns an in-process session nor elects/spawns a leader. A fresh stdio MCP process (no warm session, no bound leader) gets `IpcError::NotLeader` and there is no code path that bootstraps one — so edits can never make diagnostics appear.
-2. **Silent swallow.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs::LiveDiagnoser::diagnose` (lines 54-74) catches that typed `NotLeader` error, logs it at `debug!`, and returns `DiagnoseOutcome { report: empty, pending: false }`. `fold_outcome_into_result` then attaches nothing — indistinguishable from "code is clean." Note the irony: `produce_outcome`'s own unit test `produce_outcome_without_a_session_surfaces_the_typed_not_leader_error` (mod.rs:1001) asserts the typed error MUST surface, but the inline wrapper swallows it at the one place a human/model sees it.
+The real cause: on a cold start in a large workspace (dozens of crates), rust-analyzer is still loading the project model / indexing, and **flycheck (cargo check) has not completed a pass for this crate**, so it legitimately has published zero diagnostics yet. The quiescence/`pending` machinery — designed to flag exactly this ambiguity — concluded `settled` (`pending: false`) because it only watches for the publishDiagnostics stream to go quiet, and a server that has not published anything yet is indistinguishable from a settled-clean one.

-## Required behavior (what the user demanded)
-- **A leader-capable process must lead.** On the first mutating edit (or at server start), the process must be able to elect itself leader and warm the single rust-analyzer session, so a diagnose request has somewhere to go. "Not trying to connect/elect" is the core defect.
-- **A follower must find a leader.** When another process already holds leadership, the follower's `diagnose_via_leader` must reach it and fold real diagnostics in (this connect path exists but is currently untested end-to-end and never exercised because no leader is ever elected in the common single-process case).
-- **When it genuinely cannot diagnose, say so — don't fake clean.** In `LiveDiagnoser`'s error arm, instead of an empty outcome, fold a typed marker into the envelope (e.g. `diagnostics_unavailable: "could not reach LSP leader"`) so an empty result distinguishes "clean" from "diagnoser never ran." (This observability slice is small and independently shippable; land it + a failing real-path test first.)
+### Root cause (verified in code)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/settle.rs` — `settle`/`settle_stream` subscribe to the diagnostic publish stream and debounce on a `settle_window` of quiet; `pending: true` is only produced at the `settle_hard_timeout` backstop. It has no notion of rust-analyzer's load/index/flycheck progress, so "no publishes yet (still loading)" reads as "settled."
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/diagnose.rs:182` `diagnose_with_outcome` returns `(records, pending)` straight from `settle`; `config.rs` holds `settle_window`/`settle_hard_timeout`.
+- The test `outcome_reports_not_pending_when_settled` (diagnose.rs:628) bakes in the wrong assumption (quiet ⇒ not pending), and `produce_outcome`/the fold-in faithfully propagate the bogus `pending: false`.
+
+## Required behavior
+- An edit (or explicit diagnose) issued while rust-analyzer is still **loading/indexing**, or before **flycheck has completed at least one pass** for the target file's crate, must report **`pending: true`** (provisional), NOT a settled-empty/clean result. Only declare settled-clean once the server has actually completed a diagnostic pass and genuinely reports nothing.
+- "Clean" and "not analyzed yet" must be distinguishable in the result the model sees.

-## Approach
-- Make the edit→diagnose path establish a reachable diagnoser: on first mutating call, elect/become leader (via `swissarmyhammer-leader-election`) and spawn/warm the shared `swissarmyhammer-lsp` rust-analyzer session for the workspace, so `produce_outcome` finds an in-process session (or a reachable leader). Reuse the existing leader/session machinery the `diagnostics` MCP tool and code-context use — do not spawn a second LSP client (single-client invariant).
-- Surface the typed marker in `inline_diagnostics.rs` rather than swallowing.
-- Keep the fold-in best-effort (never fail the underlying edit), but "best-effort" must mean "attach a reason," not "silently drop."
+## Approach (investigate, then implement)
+- Make quiescence progress-aware: consume rust-analyzer's `$/progress` work-done tokens (e.g. `rustAnalyzer/Indexing`, `rustAnalyzer/cargo check`/flycheck) from the shared `swissarmyhammer-lsp` session, and/or require at least one completed flycheck pass for the file's crate, before `settle` may report not-pending. While any relevant work-done token is in progress (or no flycheck pass has completed), return `pending: true`.
+- Determine in `swissarmyhammer-lsp` whether the session already surfaces `$/progress`/work-done state; if not, plumb it through to the settle engine (without spawning a second LSP client — single-client invariant).
+- Keep `settle_hard_timeout` as the backstop, but premature quiet must no longer be read as settled while the server is provably still warming up.

+### Secondary (keep, but lower priority — NOT the observed cause)
+- The inline fold-in still swallows a genuine leader-unreachable `IpcError::NotLeader` into an empty `pending:false` envelope (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs:63-72`). That path was NOT hit in this repro, but it is a real observability gap: surface a typed `diagnostics_unavailable: "could not reach LSP leader"` marker there instead of an empty outcome, so an unreachable leader is also distinguishable from clean. Do this only as a small follow-on slice; do not re-frame the whole task around leader election (that hypothesis was disproven here).
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] After editing a file to a real compile error through the MCP `files edit` tool, the tool result envelope contains diagnostics for that error (not an empty/clean envelope) — in a single-process server with no pre-warmed session.
-- [ ] When the diagnoser truly cannot run (no leader electable, e.g. forced), the envelope carries a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` reason — never byte-identical to a clean result.
-- [ ] A follower process (leadership already held elsewhere) reaches the leader and folds the leader's real diagnostics into its edit result.
-- [ ] No second rust-analyzer/LSP client is spawned (single-client invariant preserved); the edit still succeeds even if diagnostics fail.
+- [ ] A diagnose/edit issued before rust-analyzer has completed loading/indexing or a first flycheck pass for the target crate reports `pending: true`, not settled-empty.
+- [ ] Once the server completes its pass and the file genuinely has an error, the diagnostic surfaces (folded into the edit envelope and via the explicit tool).
+- [ ] Once the server completes its pass and the file is genuinely clean, the result is settled `pending: false` with no diagnostics (true clean is still reported as clean).
+- [ ] The result distinguishes the three states — pending / clean / has-errors — at the surface the model sees.
+- [ ] (Secondary) a genuine leader-unreachable case yields a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker, not an empty clean-looking envelope.
+- [ ] No second LSP client spawned; the edit never fails because diagnostics are pending.
+
+## Tests (real-path, not mocks — the missing coverage)
+- [ ] **Core regression (fails today):** drive `settle`/`diagnose_with_outcome` with a session that is still warming up — work-done/indexing in progress (or no flycheck pass completed) and zero publishes — and assert the outcome is `pending: true`, NOT settled-empty. Use the injectable timer/manual clock already used by the settle tests.
+- [ ] Update/replace `outcome_reports_not_pending_when_settled` (diagnose.rs:628) so "quiet" alone no longer implies not-pending; a settled-not-pending result requires a completed analysis pass, not just stream silence.
+- [ ] **Settled-clean still works:** a session that completes its pass with no diagnostics yields `pending: false`, empty (no false-pending regression).
+- [ ] **End-to-end (gate on rust-analyzer installed; skip cleanly if absent):** edit a fixture file to an E0308 through the MCP edit path; assert the result is `pending` during warm-up and folds in the real diagnostic after the pass completes — never a silent clean on broken code.
+- [ ] (Secondary) forced leader-unreachable → typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics -p swissarmyhammer-tools` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`); `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.

-## Tests (the missing coverage the user called out — real-path, not mocks)
-- [ ] **Leader-capable process leads:** a test that starts from no bound leader, performs a mutating edit on a broken file, and asserts a leader/session is established and real diagnostics are folded in (end-to-end through the production fold-in, not a stubbed `MutationDiagnoser`).
-- [ ] **Follower finds leader:** with a leader already bound (its socket/lock present), a follower's `produce_outcome`/`diagnose_via_leader` returns the leader's real report and the envelope carries it.
-- [ ] **Unavailable is observable:** force the no-leader-reachable case and assert the envelope carries the typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker (regression for the silent-swallow at inline_diagnostics.rs:63-72) — this test FAILS today.
-- [ ] Gate any test that needs a real rust-analyzer behind the usual LSP-available guard (skip cleanly if rust-analyzer isn't installed), but the leader-election/marker logic must be covered without requiring the binary.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`); `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
+## Notes for the next agent (don't repeat dead ends)
+- The leader-election/"follower can't bootstrap a leader" hypothesis was investigated and **disproven** for this repro (rust-analyzer was running and reachable, the explicit tool returned empty-not-error). Start at the settle/quiescence layer, not leader election.
+- The precise spot to fix is where `settle` decides quiescence vs the server's actual load/flycheck progress.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "edit-broken-file → diagnostics appear" real-path test and the `diagnostics_unavailable` marker test FIRST (both fail today), then implement. If the leader-election/warm-up portion exceeds sizing, split it out and land the observability marker + failing real-path test as the prerequisite slice.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "warming-up ⇒ pending, not settled-empty" test first, then implement progress-aware quiescence.
\ No newline at end of file

-- The test `outcome_reports_not_pending_when_settled` (diagnose.rs:628) bakes in the wrong assumption (quiet ⇒ not pending), and `produce_outcome`/the fold-in faithfully propagate the bogus `pending: false`.
+Cross-project note: this spans `swissarmyhammer-tools` (the edit/inline path), `swissarmyhammer-diagnostics`, `swissarmyhammer-lsp` (the single rust-analyzer session), and `swissarmyhammer-leader-election`. Filed under `file-edit-tools` because the edit tool is the trigger/delivery surface; coordinate with the `diagnostics` project.

-## Required behavior
-- An edit (or explicit diagnose) issued while rust-analyzer is still **loading/indexing**, or before **flycheck has completed at least one pass** for the target file's crate, must report **`pending: true`** (provisional), NOT a settled-empty/clean result. Only declare settled-clean once the server has actually completed a diagnostic pass and genuinely reports nothing.
-- "Clean" and "not analyzed yet" must be distinguishable in the result the model sees.
+### Root cause (verified)
+1. **Pure-consumer diagnose path — never elects/warms.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/diagnostics/mod.rs::produce_outcome` (lines 365-411): finds an in-process LSP session via `lsp_session_for_file`/`any_lsp_session`; if there is none it calls `diagnose_via_leader` (lines 427-443), which only *connects* a `SessionRequestClient` to an already-bound leader socket (`workspace.socket_path()`/`lock_path()`). Neither branch spawns an in-process session nor elects/spawns a leader. A fresh stdio MCP process (no warm session, no bound leader) gets `IpcError::NotLeader` and there is no code path that bootstraps one — so edits can never make diagnostics appear.
+2. **Silent swallow.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs::LiveDiagnoser::diagnose` (lines 54-74) catches that typed `NotLeader` error, logs it at `debug!`, and returns `DiagnoseOutcome { report: empty, pending: false }`. `fold_outcome_into_result` then attaches nothing — indistinguishable from "code is clean." Note the irony: `produce_outcome`'s own unit test `produce_outcome_without_a_session_surfaces_the_typed_not_leader_error` (mod.rs:1001) asserts the typed error MUST surface, but the inline wrapper swallows it at the one place a human/model sees it.

-## Approach (investigate, then implement)
-- Make quiescence progress-aware: consume rust-analyzer's `$/progress` work-done tokens (e.g. `rustAnalyzer/Indexing`, `rustAnalyzer/cargo check`/flycheck) from the shared `swissarmyhammer-lsp` session, and/or require at least one completed flycheck pass for the file's crate, before `settle` may report not-pending. While any relevant work-done token is in progress (or no flycheck pass has completed), return `pending: true`.
-- Determine in `swissarmyhammer-lsp` whether the session already surfaces `$/progress`/work-done state; if not, plumb it through to the settle engine (without spawning a second LSP client — single-client invariant).
-- Keep `settle_hard_timeout` as the backstop, but premature quiet must no longer be read as settled while the server is provably still warming up.
+## Required behavior (what the user demanded)
+- **A leader-capable process must lead.** On the first mutating edit (or at server start), the process must be able to elect itself leader and warm the single rust-analyzer session, so a diagnose request has somewhere to go. "Not trying to connect/elect" is the core defect.
+- **A follower must find a leader.** When another process already holds leadership, the follower's `diagnose_via_leader` must reach it and fold real diagnostics in (this connect path exists but is currently untested end-to-end and never exercised because no leader is ever elected in the common single-process case).
+- **When it genuinely cannot diagnose, say so — don't fake clean.** In `LiveDiagnoser`'s error arm, instead of an empty outcome, fold a typed marker into the envelope (e.g. `diagnostics_unavailable: "could not reach LSP leader"`) so an empty result distinguishes "clean" from "diagnoser never ran." (This observability slice is small and independently shippable; land it + a failing real-path test first.)

-### Secondary (keep, but lower priority — NOT the observed cause)
-- The inline fold-in still swallows a genuine leader-unreachable `IpcError::NotLeader` into an empty `pending:false` envelope (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs:63-72`). That path was NOT hit in this repro, but it is a real observability gap: surface a typed `diagnostics_unavailable: "could not reach LSP leader"` marker there instead of an empty outcome, so an unreachable leader is also distinguishable from clean. Do this only as a small follow-on slice; do not re-frame the whole task around leader election (that hypothesis was disproven here).
+## Approach
+- Make the edit→diagnose path establish a reachable diagnoser: on first mutating call, elect/become leader (via `swissarmyhammer-leader-election`) and spawn/warm the shared `swissarmyhammer-lsp` rust-analyzer session for the workspace, so `produce_outcome` finds an in-process session (or a reachable leader). Reuse the existing leader/session machinery the `diagnostics` MCP tool and code-context use — do not spawn a second LSP client (single-client invariant).
+- Surface the typed marker in `inline_diagnostics.rs` rather than swallowing.
+- Keep the fold-in best-effort (never fail the underlying edit), but "best-effort" must mean "attach a reason," not "silently drop."

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A diagnose/edit issued before rust-analyzer has completed loading/indexing or a first flycheck pass for the target crate reports `pending: true`, not settled-empty.
-- [ ] Once the server completes its pass and the file genuinely has an error, the diagnostic surfaces (folded into the edit envelope and via the explicit tool).
-- [ ] Once the server completes its pass and the file is genuinely clean, the result is settled `pending: false` with no diagnostics (true clean is still reported as clean).
-- [ ] The result distinguishes the three states — pending / clean / has-errors — at the surface the model sees.
-- [ ] (Secondary) a genuine leader-unreachable case yields a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker, not an empty clean-looking envelope.
-- [ ] No second LSP client spawned; the edit never fails because diagnostics are pending.
+- [ ] After editing a file to a real compile error through the MCP `files edit` tool, the tool result envelope contains diagnostics for that error (not an empty/clean envelope) — in a single-process server with no pre-warmed session.
+- [ ] When the diagnoser truly cannot run (no leader electable, e.g. forced), the envelope carries a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` reason — never byte-identical to a clean result.
+- [ ] A follower process (leadership already held elsewhere) reaches the leader and folds the leader's real diagnostics into its edit result.
+- [ ] No second rust-analyzer/LSP client is spawned (single-client invariant preserved); the edit still succeeds even if diagnostics fail.

-## Tests (real-path, not mocks — the missing coverage)
-- [ ] **Core regression (fails today):** drive `settle`/`diagnose_with_outcome` with a session that is still warming up — work-done/indexing in progress (or no flycheck pass completed) and zero publishes — and assert the outcome is `pending: true`, NOT settled-empty. Use the injectable timer/manual clock already used by the settle tests.
-- [ ] Update/replace `outcome_reports_not_pending_when_settled` (diagnose.rs:628) so "quiet" alone no longer implies not-pending; a settled-not-pending result requires a completed analysis pass, not just stream silence.
-- [ ] **Settled-clean still works:** a session that completes its pass with no diagnostics yields `pending: false`, empty (no false-pending regression).
-- [ ] **End-to-end (gate on rust-analyzer installed; skip cleanly if absent):** edit a fixture file to an E0308 through the MCP edit path; assert the result is `pending` during warm-up and folds in the real diagnostic after the pass completes — never a silent clean on broken code.
-- [ ] (Secondary) forced leader-unreachable → typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics -p swissarmyhammer-tools` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`); `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
-
-## Notes for the next agent (don't repeat dead ends)
-- The leader-election/"follower can't bootstrap a leader" hypothesis was investigated and **disproven** for this repro (rust-analyzer was running and reachable, the explicit tool returned empty-not-error). Start at the settle/quiescence layer, not leader election.
-- The precise spot to fix is where `settle` decides quiescence vs the server's actual load/flycheck progress.
+## Tests (the missing coverage the user called out — real-path, not mocks)
+- [ ] **Leader-capable process leads:** a test that starts from no bound leader, performs a mutating edit on a broken file, and asserts a leader/session is established and real diagnostics are folded in (end-to-end through the production fold-in, not a stubbed `MutationDiagnoser`).
+- [ ] **Follower finds leader:** with a leader already bound (its socket/lock present), a follower's `produce_outcome`/`diagnose_via_leader` returns the leader's real report and the envelope carries it.
+- [ ] **Unavailable is observable:** force the no-leader-reachable case and assert the envelope carries the typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker (regression for the silent-swallow at inline_diagnostics.rs:63-72) — this test FAILS today.
+- [ ] Gate any test that needs a real rust-analyzer behind the usual LSP-available guard (skip cleanly if rust-analyzer isn't installed), but the leader-election/marker logic must be covered without requiring the binary.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`); `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "warming-up ⇒ pending, not settled-empty" test first, then implement progress-aware quiescence.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "edit-broken-file → diagnostics appear" real-path test and the `diagnostics_unavailable` marker test FIRST (both fail today), then implement. If the leader-election/warm-up portion exceeds sizing, split it out and land the observability marker + failing real-path test as the prerequisite slice.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,57 +0,0 @@
----
-assignees:
-- claude-code
-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: a180
-project: file-edit-tools
-title: Diagnostics quiescence declares "settled/clean" while rust-analyzer is still loading/indexing — can't distinguish clean from not-analyzed-yet
----
-## What
-The diagnostics system cannot tell "the code is clean" from "rust-analyzer hasn't analyzed it yet" — both render as 0 errors with `pending: false`. After an edit that introduces a real compile error (E0308), the inline fold-in AND the explicit `diagnostics` tool both return empty-and-not-pending, so the model/user sees "looks fine" on genuinely-broken code.
-
-### Evidence (live repro — supersedes an earlier WRONG hypothesis)
-An earlier diagnosis blamed leader-election ("follower can't reach/elect a leader; NotLeader silently swallowed"). **That was wrong for the observed case and should not be chased as the root cause:**
-- `list servers` showed rust-analyzer running (fresh pid), and the explicit `diagnostics` check returned `0 errors` **empty — NOT** a "could not reach leader" error. So the leader was reachable and a session existed.
-- A second breaking edit produced an identical empty envelope; waiting 12s and re-checking still gave 0 errors.
-
-The real cause: on a cold start in a large workspace (dozens of crates), rust-analyzer is still loading the project model / indexing, and **flycheck (cargo check) has not completed a pass for this crate**, so it legitimately has published zero diagnostics yet. The quiescence/`pending` machinery — designed to flag exactly this ambiguity — concluded `settled` (`pending: false`) because it only watches for the publishDiagnostics stream to go quiet, and a server that has not published anything yet is indistinguishable from a settled-clean one.
-
-### Root cause (verified in code)
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/settle.rs` — `settle`/`settle_stream` subscribe to the diagnostic publish stream and debounce on a `settle_window` of quiet; `pending: true` is only produced at the `settle_hard_timeout` backstop. It has no notion of rust-analyzer's load/index/flycheck progress, so "no publishes yet (still loading)" reads as "settled."
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/diagnose.rs:182` `diagnose_with_outcome` returns `(records, pending)` straight from `settle`; `config.rs` holds `settle_window`/`settle_hard_timeout`.
-- The test `outcome_reports_not_pending_when_settled` (diagnose.rs:628) bakes in the wrong assumption (quiet ⇒ not pending), and `produce_outcome`/the fold-in faithfully propagate the bogus `pending: false`.
-
-## Required behavior
-- An edit (or explicit diagnose) issued while rust-analyzer is still **loading/indexing**, or before **flycheck has completed at least one pass** for the target file's crate, must report **`pending: true`** (provisional), NOT a settled-empty/clean result. Only declare settled-clean once the server has actually completed a diagnostic pass and genuinely reports nothing.
-- "Clean" and "not analyzed yet" must be distinguishable in the result the model sees.
-
-## Approach (investigate, then implement)
-- Make quiescence progress-aware: consume rust-analyzer's `$/progress` work-done tokens (e.g. `rustAnalyzer/Indexing`, `rustAnalyzer/cargo check`/flycheck) from the shared `swissarmyhammer-lsp` session, and/or require at least one completed flycheck pass for the file's crate, before `settle` may report not-pending. While any relevant work-done token is in progress (or no flycheck pass has completed), return `pending: true`.
-- Determine in `swissarmyhammer-lsp` whether the session already surfaces `$/progress`/work-done state; if not, plumb it through to the settle engine (without spawning a second LSP client — single-client invariant).
-- Keep `settle_hard_timeout` as the backstop, but premature quiet must no longer be read as settled while the server is provably still warming up.
-
-### Secondary (keep, but lower priority — NOT the observed cause)
-- The inline fold-in still swallows a genuine leader-unreachable `IpcError::NotLeader` into an empty `pending:false` envelope (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs:63-72`). That path was NOT hit in this repro, but it is a real observability gap: surface a typed `diagnostics_unavailable: "could not reach LSP leader"` marker there instead of an empty outcome, so an unreachable leader is also distinguishable from clean. Do this only as a small follow-on slice; do not re-frame the whole task around leader election (that hypothesis was disproven here).
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A diagnose/edit issued before rust-analyzer has completed loading/indexing or a first flycheck pass for the target crate reports `pending: true`, not settled-empty.
-- [ ] Once the server completes its pass and the file genuinely has an error, the diagnostic surfaces (folded into the edit envelope and via the explicit tool).
-- [ ] Once the server completes its pass and the file is genuinely clean, the result is settled `pending: false` with no diagnostics (true clean is still reported as clean).
-- [ ] The result distinguishes the three states — pending / clean / has-errors — at the surface the model sees.
-- [ ] (Secondary) a genuine leader-unreachable case yields a typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker, not an empty clean-looking envelope.
-- [ ] No second LSP client spawned; the edit never fails because diagnostics are pending.
-
-## Tests (real-path, not mocks — the missing coverage)
-- [ ] **Core regression (fails today):** drive `settle`/`diagnose_with_outcome` with a session that is still warming up — work-done/indexing in progress (or no flycheck pass completed) and zero publishes — and assert the outcome is `pending: true`, NOT settled-empty. Use the injectable timer/manual clock already used by the settle tests.
-- [ ] Update/replace `outcome_reports_not_pending_when_settled` (diagnose.rs:628) so "quiet" alone no longer implies not-pending; a settled-not-pending result requires a completed analysis pass, not just stream silence.
-- [ ] **Settled-clean still works:** a session that completes its pass with no diagnostics yields `pending: false`, empty (no false-pending regression).
-- [ ] **End-to-end (gate on rust-analyzer installed; skip cleanly if absent):** edit a fixture file to an E0308 through the MCP edit path; assert the result is `pending` during warm-up and folds in the real diagnostic after the pass completes — never a silent clean on broken code.
-- [ ] (Secondary) forced leader-unreachable → typed `diagnostics_unavailable` marker.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-diagnostics -p swissarmyhammer-tools` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`); `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
-
-## Notes for the next agent (don't repeat dead ends)
-- The leader-election/"follower can't bootstrap a leader" hypothesis was investigated and **disproven** for this repro (rust-analyzer was running and reachable, the explicit tool returned empty-not-error). Start at the settle/quiescence layer, not leader election.
-- The precise spot to fix is where `settle` decides quiescence vs the server's actual load/flycheck progress.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "warming-up ⇒ pending, not settled-empty" test first, then implement progress-aware quiescence.
\ No newline at end of file

+- Determine in `swissarmyhammer-lsp` whether the session already surfaces `$/progress`/work-done state; if not, plumb it through to the settle engine (without spawning a second LSP client — single-client invariant).
+- Keep `settle_hard_timeout` as the backstop, but premature quiet must no longer be read as settled while the server is provably still warming up.
